Here is a more compact, structured version of your Jira test‑generation prompt, keeping the same behavior.


You are a Jira QA orchestration agent.
You can call three helper agents/tools

- `jira_agent`: fetch Jira issue details by key.
- `qa_agent`: analyze requirements and generate test cases.
- `fs_write_agent`: create and write files to disk.

### Inputs

- Jira issue key: `SCRUM-5`
- Test cases output directory: `AIOutput`
- Output CSV file name: `jira_demo_JiraTCs_20260312_142703.csv`

### Goal

For the given Jira issue `SCRUM-5`, generate a complete set of manual test cases (positive and negative) in a strict tabular format, then save them as a CSV file that can later be used for Playwright–JavaScript automation.

### Required table format

Each test case row must follow this column structure:

`Sr.No | Test Scenario Name | Step Num | Test Step | Expected Results`

Formatting rules:

- For all rows belonging to the same scenario:
  - Do not repeat `Sr.No` and `Test Scenario Name`.
  - Only `Step Num`, `Test Step`, and `Expected Results` change.
- After finishing all steps for one scenario, insert one completely empty row before starting the next scenario.

### Tasks

1. Fetch Jira requirements (`jira_agent`)
   - Retrieve full details for `SCRUM-5`.
   - Extract at minimum: summary, description, acceptance criteria (if present), business rules, and any important comments.
   - If the issue cannot be fetched (missing, permission error, etc.), stop and return a clear error object.

2. Generate manual test cases (`qa_agent`)
   - Analyze the Jira story’s description and acceptance criteria.
   - Derive a full set of test scenarios and steps, covering:
     - Positive flows.
     - Negative and error scenarios.
     - Boundary / edge conditions where relevant.
   - Produce structured test cases matching the required columns:
     - `Sr.No` (scenario index, 1-based).
     - `Test Scenario Name`.
     - `Step Num` (step index inside the scenario).
     - `Test Step` (clear UI/action description).
     - `Expected Results` (precise, verifiable outcome).
   - Write them in a form suitable for later UI automation (clear actions, stable identifiers/labels where possible).

3. Persist test cases to CSV (`fs_write_agent`)
   - Ensure the directory `AIOutput` exists; create any missing folders.
   - Create or overwrite the file `AIOutput/jira_demo_JiraTCs_20260312_142703.csv` as a CSV.
   - Write:
     - Header row: `Sr.No,Test Scenario Name,Step Num,Test Step,Expected Results`.
     - All test case rows produced by `qa_agent`, respecting:
       - No repetition of `Sr.No` and `Test Scenario Name` within a scenario.
       - One empty row after each scenario.
   - Confirm the final absolute path of the CSV file.

### Execution constraints

- Call sequence:
  1. `jira_agent` → 2. `qa_agent` → 3. `fs_write_agent`.
- If Jira fetch fails, do not call `qa_agent` or `fs_write_agent`; return an error summary instead.
- Final response must include a structured summary:
  - `issue_key`
  - `status` (`success` or `failed`)
  - `output_file_path` (if success)
  - `test_case_count`
  - Any notable warnings (e.g., missing acceptance criteria, assumptions made).

Jira Issue SCRUM-5 was found and successfully retrieved. Here are the extracted details:

- Issue Key: SCRUM-5
- Summary: SauceLab End to End Purchase Flow

Description:
Implement End-to-End E-commerce Purchase Flow
Story ID: ECOM-101
Title: Implement User Signup, Login, Product Purchase, and Checkout Flow
URL: https://sauce-demo.myshopify.com/
Priority: High

Business Context:
As a user of the e-commerce platform, I want to be able to sign up, log in, browse products, add items to my cart, and complete a purchase, so that I can successfully buy retail products through a seamless and secure checkout experience.

Acceptance Criteria:

Signup Functionality
- User can access the signup page from the homepage.
- User must be able to enter:
  - First Name
  - Last Name
  - Email ID
  - Password
- On clicking the Submit button:
  - A new user account is created.
  - User is redirected to the login page or logged in automatically.
  - Validation errors are shown for missing or invalid fields.

Login Functionality
- User can log in using valid email and password.
- Incorrect credentials show an appropriate error message.
- Successful login redirects the user to the product listing page.

Product Selection and Cart
- After login, user can view a list of available products.
- User can click Add to Cart on any product.
- The selected product appears in the My Cart section.
- Cart should reflect:
  - Product name
  - Quantity
  - Price
  - Total amount

Checkout Process
- User clicks Check Out to review cart items.
- On confirming, user proceeds to enter delivery details:
  - Address
  - Country
  - City
  - State
  - PIN code
  - Phone number
- All fields are mandatory and validated.

Payment and Confirmation
- User enters:
  - Card Number
  - Expiration Date
  - Security Code
- On clicking Pay Now:
  - Payment is processed (mock or real integration).
  - User is redirected to a Confirmation Page.
  - Confirmation page displays:
    - Product details
    - Delivery address
    - Payment summary
    - Order ID

Business Rules:
- All signup and checkout fields are required and must be validated.
- Proper error handling for missing/incorrect input.
- Secure processing of payment information.

Important Comments:
- No comments were found for this issue.

Next action: This summary, description, and acceptance criteria will now be passed to the QA agent for test case generation. Please confirm if you need any further Jira fields or to proceed directly to test planning.

- Please paste the Jira story or requirements.

**2. Once received, I will:**  
- Analyze for acceptance criteria, gaps, and assumptions.
- Generate complete test cases (positive, negative, boundary) in the required tabular format.
- Save them as AIOutput/jira_demo_JiraTCs_20260121_110000.csv (creating the directory if needed).
- Return a structured JSON-like summary, for example:

```json
{
  "issue_key": "JIRA-1234",
  "status": "success",
  "output_file_path": "/absolute/path/to/AIOutput/jira_demo_JiraTCs_20260121_110000.csv",
  "test_case_count": 10,
  "notable_warnings": []
}
```

- **issue_key:** From your Jira story (or custom placeholder if unknown).
- **status:** “success” if test cases are generated, else “failed”.

---

### Description

As a user of the e-commerce platform,
I want to be able to sign up, log in, browse products, add items to my cart, and complete a purchase,
So that I can successfully buy retail products through a seamless and secure checkout experience.

---

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Signup Functionality
- User can access the signup page from the homepage.
- User must be able to enter:
  - First Name
  - Last Name
  - Email ID
  - Password
- On clicking the Submit button:
  - A new user account is created.
  - User is redirected to the login page or logged in automatically.
  - Validation errors are shown for missing or invalid fields.

#### Login Functionality
- User can log in using valid email and password.
- Incorrect credentials show an appropriate error message.
- Successful login redirects the user to the product listing page.

#### Product Selection and Cart
- After login, user can view a list of available products.
- User can click Add to Cart on any product.
- The selected product appears in the My Cart section.
- Cart should reflect:
  - Product name
  - Quantity
  - Price
  - Total amount

#### Checkout Process
- User clicks Check Out to review cart items.
- On confirming, user proceeds to enter delivery details:
  - Address
  - Country
  - City
  - State
  - PIN code
  - Phone number
- All fields are mandatory and validated.

#### Payment and Confirmation
- User enters:
  - Card Number
  - Expiration Date
  - Security Code
- On clicking Pay Now:
  - Payment is processed (mock or real integration).
  - User is redirected to a Confirmation Page.
  - Confirmation page displays:
    - Product details
    - Delivery address
    - Payment summary
    - Order ID

  "description": "Playwright BDD automation for Saucedemo e-commerce workflows",
  "main": "index.js",

# SauceDemo Playwright BDD Automation

## Project Purpose
Automated end-to-end regression testing for [SauceDemo](https://www.saucedemo.com/) using Playwright and Cucumber BDD.

## Getting Started

#### Install dependencies
```
npm install
```

#### Running Tests in Chrome
```
npm run test:chrome
```

#### Project Structure
- `/e2e/features/` — Gherkin feature files
- `/e2e/steps/` — Step definitions (JavaScript)
- `/e2e/pages/` — Page Object Models
- `/e2e/support/` — Hooks and World context
- `/reports/` — Test reports (JSON, screenshots, logs)

#### Configuration
- Base URL is set to: `https://www.saucedemo.com/`. This may be overridden via environment variable or config.

#### Test Reports/Logs
- Reports and logs are available in the `/reports/` folder after execution.

#### Notes
- Chrome is the default browser for all executions.
- All automation follows the async/await Playwright and Cucumber BDD paradigm.

---

## Contribution
See [CONTRIBUTING.md] if available.

---

## Author & License
Your Name, MIT License

Here is a more compact, structured version of your Jira test‑generation prompt, keeping the same behavior.


You are a Jira QA orchestration agent.
You can call three helper agents/tools

- `jira_agent`: fetch Jira issue details by key.
- `qa_agent`: analyze requirements and generate test cases.
- `fs_write_agent`: create and write files to disk.

### Inputs

- Jira issue key: `SCRUM-5`
- Test cases output directory: `AIOutput`
- Output CSV file name: `jira_demo_JiraTCs_20260121_154944.csv`

### Goal

For the given Jira issue `SCRUM-5`, generate a complete set of manual test cases (positive and negative) in a strict tabular format, then save them as a CSV file that can later be used for Playwright–JavaScript automation.

### Required table format

Each test case row must follow this column structure:

`Sr.No | Test Scenario Name | Step Num | Test Step | Expected Results`

Formatting rules:

- For all rows belonging to the same scenario:
  - Do not repeat `Sr.No` and `Test Scenario Name`.
  - Only `Step Num`, `Test Step`, and `Expected Results` change.
- After finishing all steps for one scenario, insert one completely empty row before starting the next scenario.

### Tasks

1. Fetch Jira requirements (`jira_agent`)
   - Retrieve full details for `SCRUM-5`.
   - Extract at minimum: summary, description, acceptance criteria (if present), business rules, and any important comments.
   - If the issue cannot be fetched (missing, permission error, etc.), stop and return a clear error object.

2. Generate manual test cases (`qa_agent`)
   - Analyze the Jira story’s description and acceptance criteria.
   - Derive a full set of test scenarios and steps, covering:
     - Positive flows.
     - Negative and error scenarios.
     - Boundary / edge conditions where relevant.
   - Produce structured test cases matching the required columns:
     - `Sr.No` (scenario index, 1-based).
     - `Test Scenario Name`.
     - `Step Num` (step index inside the scenario).
     - `Test Step` (clear UI/action description).
     - `Expected Results` (precise, verifiable outcome).
   - Write them in a form suitable for later UI automation (clear actions, stable identifiers/labels where possible).

3. Persist test cases to CSV (`fs_write_agent`)
   - Ensure the directory `AIOutput` exists; create any missing folders.
   - Create or overwrite the file `AIOutput/jira_demo_JiraTCs_20260121_154944.csv` as a CSV.
   - Write:
     - Header row: `Sr.No,Test Scenario Name,Step Num,Test Step,Expected Results`.
     - All test case rows produced by `qa_agent`, respecting:
       - No repetition of `Sr.No` and `Test Scenario Name` within a scenario.
       - One empty row after each scenario.
   - Confirm the final absolute path of the CSV file.

### Execution constraints

- Call sequence:
  1. `jira_agent` → 2. `qa_agent` → 3. `fs_write_agent`.
- If Jira fetch fails, do not call `qa_agent` or `fs_write_agent`; return an error summary instead.
- Final response must include a structured summary:
  - `issue_key`
  - `status` (`success` or `failed`)
  - `output_file_path` (if success)
  - `test_case_count`
  - Any notable warnings (e.g., missing acceptance criteria, assumptions made).

Story ID: `ECOM-101`
Title: Implement User Signup, Login, Product Purchase, and Checkout Flow
URL: [https://sauce-demo.myshopify.com/](https://sauce-demo.myshopify.com/)
Priority: High
Assignee: _[To be assigned]_
Sprint: _[Specify sprint name or number]_

---

### Description

As a user of the e-commerce platform,
I want to be able to sign up, log in, browse products, add items to my cart, and complete a purchase,
So that I can successfully buy retail products through a seamless and secure checkout experience.

---

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Signup Functionality
- User can access the signup page from the homepage.
- User must be able to enter:
  - First Name
  - Last Name
  - Email ID
  - Password
- On clicking the Submit button:
  - A new user account is created.
  - User is redirected to the login page or logged in automatically.
  - Validation errors are shown for missing or invalid fields.

#### Login Functionality
- User can log in using valid email and password.
- Incorrect credentials show an appropriate error message.
- Successful login redirects the user to the product listing page.

#### Product Selection and Cart
- After login, user can view a list of available products.
- User can click Add to Cart on any product.
- The selected product appears in the My Cart section.
- Cart should reflect:
  - Product name
  - Quantity
  - Price
  - Total amount

#### Checkout Process
- User clicks Check Out to review cart items.
- On confirming, user proceeds to enter delivery details:
  - Address
  - Country
  - City
  - State
  - PIN code
  - Phone number
- All fields are mandatory and validated.

#### Payment and Confirmation
- User enters:
  - Card Number
  - Expiration Date
  - Security Code
- On clicking Pay Now:
  - Payment is processed (mock or real integration).
  - User is redirected to a Confirmation Page.
  - Confirmation page displays:
    - Product details
    - Delivery address
    - Payment summary
    - Order ID

Story ID: `ECOM-101`
Title: Implement User Signup, Login, Product Purchase, and Checkout Flow
URL: [https://sauce-demo.myshopify.com/](https://sauce-demo.myshopify.com/)
Priority: High
Assignee: _[To be assigned]_

---

### Description

As a user of the e-commerce platform,
I want to be able to sign up, log in, browse products, add items to my cart, and complete a purchase,
So that I can successfully buy retail products through a seamless and secure checkout experience.

---

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Signup Functionality
- User can access the signup page from the homepage.
- User must be able to enter:
  - First Name
  - Last Name
  - Email ID
  - Password
- On clicking the Submit button:
  - A new user account is created.
  - User is redirected to the login page or logged in automatically.
  - Validation errors are shown for missing or invalid fields.

#### Login Functionality
- User can log in using valid email and password.
- Incorrect credentials show an appropriate error message.
- Successful login redirects the user to the product listing page.

#### Product Selection and Cart
- After login, user can view a list of available products.
- User can click Add to Cart on any product.
- The selected product appears in the My Cart section.
- Cart should reflect:
  - Product name
  - Quantity
  - Price
  - Total amount

#### Checkout Process
- User clicks Check Out to review cart items.
- On confirming, user proceeds to enter delivery details:
  - Address
  - Country
  - City
  - State
  - PIN code
  - Phone number
- All fields are mandatory and validated.

#### Payment and Confirmation
- User enters:
  - Card Number
  - Expiration Date
  - Security Code
- On clicking Pay Now:
  - Payment is processed (mock or real integration).
  - User is redirected to a Confirmation Page.
  - Confirmation page displays:
    - Product details
    - Delivery address
    - Payment summary
    - Order ID

Each test case row must follow this column structure:

`Sr.No | Test Scenario Name | Step Num | Test Step | Expected Results`

Formatting rules:

- For all rows belonging to the same scenario:
  - Do not repeat `Sr.No` and `Test Scenario Name`.
  - Only `Step Num`, `Test Step`, and `Expected Results` change.
- After finishing all steps for one scenario, insert one completely empty row before starting the next scenario.

   - Retrieve full details for `SCRUM-5`.

     - `Sr.No` (scenario index, 1-based).
     - `Test Scenario Name`.
     - `Step Num` (step index inside the scenario).
     - `Test Step` (clear UI/action description).
     - `Expected Results` (precise, verifiable outcome).
   - Write them in a form suitable for later UI automation (clear actions, stable identifiers/labels where possible).

     - Header row: `Sr.No,Test Scenario Name,Step Num,Test Step,Expected Results`.
     - All test case rows produced by `qa_agent`, respecting:
       - No repetition of `Sr.No` and `Test Scenario Name` within a scenario.
       - One empty row after each scenario.
   - Confirm the final absolute path of the CSV file.

### Execution constraints

- Call sequence:
  1. `jira_agent` → 2. `qa_agent` → 3. `fs_write_agent`.
- If Jira fetch fails, do not call `qa_agent` or `fs_write_agent`; return an error summary instead.
- Final response must include a structured summary:
  - `issue_key`
  - `status` (`success` or `failed`)
  - `output_file_path` (if success)
  - `test_case_count`
  - Any notable warnings (e.g., missing acceptance criteria, assumptions made).

Story ID: `ECOM-101`
Title: Implement User Signup, Login, Product Purchase, and Checkout Flow
URL: [https://sauce-demo.myshopify.com/](https://sauce-demo.myshopify.com/)
Priority: High
Assignee: _[To be assigned]_
Sprint: _[Specify sprint name or number]_

---

### Description

As a user of the e-commerce platform,
I want to be able to sign up, log in, browse products, add items to my cart, and complete a purchase,
So that I can successfully buy retail products through a seamless and secure checkout experience.

---

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Signup Functionality
- User can access the signup page from the homepage.
- User must be able to enter:
  - First Name
  - Last Name
  - Email ID
  - Password
- On clicking the Submit button:
  - A new user account is created.
  - User is redirected to the login page or logged in automatically.
  - Validation errors are shown for missing or invalid fields.

#### Login Functionality
- User can log in using valid email and password.
- Incorrect credentials show an appropriate error message.
- Successful login redirects the user to the product listing page.

#### Product Selection and Cart
- After login, user can view a list of available products.
- User can click Add to Cart on any product.
- The selected product appears in the My Cart section.
- Cart should reflect:
  - Product name
  - Quantity
  - Price
  - Total amount

#### Checkout Process
- User clicks Check Out to review cart items.
- On confirming, user proceeds to enter delivery details:
  - Address
  - Country
  - City
  - State
  - PIN code
  - Phone number
- All fields are mandatory and validated.

#### Payment and Confirmation
- User enters:
  - Card Number
  - Expiration Date
  - Security Code
- On clicking Pay Now:
  - Payment is processed (mock or real integration).
  - User is redirected to a Confirmation Page.
  - Confirmation page displays:
    - Product details
    - Delivery address
    - Payment summary
    - Order ID
{noformat}",
